top_signals.title: FTSE 100 Live: Stocks slump after markets rocked by tech-sell off; US claims ‘good foundations’ of Iran deal
top_signals.summary: Welcome back to the City AM liveblog. Markets have shrugged off the latest developments in US-Iran peace with the FTSE 100 falling at open as London got swept up in the wider sell-off that rocked Asia and Wall Street overnight. This comes despite some progress in the Middle East, with Trump’s Vice President JD Vance [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: SpaceX’s $600 billion plunge erased equivalent of nearly half of bitcoin’s market cap in three days
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Masayoshi Son dismisses Musk’s idea for orbital data centers
top_signals.summary: The main advantage of building data centers in space would be to slash electricity costs, but such expenses comprise a small fraction of total costs.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Burnham to lay out economic plan, but markets fear Miliband as Chancellor
top_signals.summary: With more than three weeks to go before he can formally clinch the keys to 10 Downing Street, leadership challenger Andy Burnham is planning to make a major speech on the economy next week.  It’s part of an effort to reassure the bond markets – to which he once said we shouldn’t be “in hock” [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
